question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $120,000 on November 7?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: Binance BTCUSDT Candlestick - Kaggle
evidences.content: ## About Dataset # 📊 Binance BTCUSDT Candlestick This dataset contains 1-hour candlestick (OHLCV) data for the BTC/USDT trading pair on the Binance exchange. It captures market behavior over time and is ideal for data scientists, traders, and researchers performing quantitative analysis, time series forecasting, or strategy backtesting. ## 🧠 Dataset Overview Each row represents a 1-hour candlestick interval, including: Open Time and Close Time (in milliseconds) Open, High, Low, Close prices (OHLC) Trade volume and quote volume Number of trades Base volume (taker volume) ## 📌 Column Descriptions [...] ## 📌 Column Descriptions | Column Name | Description | --- | | `kline_ot` | Kline Open Time (timestamp in ms since epoch) | | `open_price` | Price at the beginning of the candle | | `high_price` | Highest price within the candle interval | | `low_price` | Lowest price within the candle interval | | `close_price` | Price at the end of the candle | | `volume` | Total trading volume in base currency (BTC) | | `kline_ct` | Kline Close Time (timestamp in ms) | | `quote_volume` | Volume in quote currency (USDT) | | `num_trades` | Number of executed trades | | `base_volume` | Taker buy bas...

evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date range and ...
evidences.content: ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:45 Petr Hejda's user avatar Petr Hejda

evidences.title: Binance Market Data - Amberdata
evidences.content: Our spot data for Binance goes back to 2017-08-17 and for Binance futures to 2019-09-08. We do not cover Binance US. For the OHLCV values, the price is always in quote and the volume unit is always in base. For example, if the pair was BTC-USD, then the price values returned are in USD and the volume value is in BTC. Our OHLCV endpoints are available via REST API for historical (time series) data as well as WebSockets for real-time data. Here is a sample file. ### Binance OHLCV API Endpoints #### Spot /market/spot/ohlcv/information /market/spot/ohlcv/{pair}/latest /market/spot/ohlcv/{exchange}/exchange/latest /market/spot/ohlcv/{pair}/historical /market/spot/ohlcv/{exchange}/exchange/historical #### Futures /market/futures/ohlcv/information [...] ## Explore Binance Data on AmberLens AmberLens offers interactive dashboards featuring comprehensive Binance data, providing users with valuable insights into market trends, trading volumes, and price movements. These dashboards enable real-time analysis and visualization of Binance's market data, supporting traders and analysts in making informed decisions. Users can explore detailed metrics and historical data through user-friendly in...

This finding, reported by Cointelegraph and based on data from the crypto analytics platform TokenInsight, highlights a pivotal shift in market structure as of early 2025. Meanwhile, the derivatives landscape shows a different leader, with Bitget ranking highest for futures and perpetual swap liquidity. [...] The price of Bitcoin (BTC) on Binance is based on live market data from active trading pairs involving Bitcoin. Prices may reflect recent trades, available liquidity, order book activity, and market demand across supported pairs such as BTC/USDT, BTC/USDC, or other available markets. evidences.title: A dive into liquidity demographics for crypto asset trading | S&P Global
evidences.content: Figure 3 summarizes the similarities and differences between TradFi exchanges, CEXs and DEXs. In the remainder of the report, we calculate liquidity metrics for selected markets that include three stablecoins (USDC, USDT, DAI) and two cryptocurrencies (BTC and ETH). We looked at the historical data from Jan 1, 2023, to Feb. 28, 2025. For CEXs, we use data from Binance, and for DEXs, we use data from Uniswap V3. Each of the pairs we analyze represents a standalone segment of the crypto market that trades on a specific exchange and exhibits certain liquidity characteristics. [...] Figure 17 shows the market depth on Binance for the asset pair EUR-USDT in euros for the 1% and 10% band (up and down) from the midpoint of the bid-ask price. Of note is the shallow market of less than €5 million for the 1% band and less than €8 million for the cumulative 10% band. This is not surprising as stablecoins have more utility on-chain than off-chain. By contrast, on Binance, USDT liquidity is much greater against other digital assets such as ETH and BTC than against fiat markets.Figure 18 shows a much higher market depth for BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT for the 1% band around the mid-price of USDT 15...

While prices were range-bound in 2019, Bitcoin began a rally post-pandemic, increasing by 1,600% to a high of $68,900 in November 2021. evidences.content: Chart structure suggests Bitcoin may be approaching a stabilization zone. A decisive drop toward $84,000 could set the stage for a technical reaction if buyers step in with conviction. Traders will be watching for signatures of exhaustion: a bullish engulfing candle, long lower shadows, or an RSI divergence against new lows. If BTC rebounds, the first checkpoint lies near $96,000, followed by a potential climb toward $111,000, where a cluster of past rejections and the broken trendline converge. A close above this band would rebuild bullish momentum and open the path back to the $124,000 ceiling. BTC Price Forecast: Can Bulls Regain Control? [...] Image 5_Bitcoin Price Chart – Source: Tradingview_ The correction is driving BTC directly into a broad $83,800–$90,000 demand zone, an area that previously fueled April’s recovery. The region overlaps with the 0.618 Fibonacci retracement, strengthening its importance for both tactical buyers and long-term allocators. evidences.title: Secret weapon "Mastering Time Zones" | Trade Oracle X ...
evidences.content: ### 1. Asian Session (UTC 00:00–08:00) 🌸 Who’s Trading? Japan, South Korea, China, and Southeast Asia dominate. Think Binance’s massive APAC user base. What’s Hot? Pairs like BTC/JPY, ETH/USDT, and BNB/USDT see heavy action. Altcoins often pump first here. Why It Matters: Asian markets often set the daily trend. News like China’s regulatory moves or South Korea’s exchange hacks can spark wild volatility. Pro Tip: Follow Binance Square for Asian market sentiment. Use Binance’s K-line charts to spot early breakouts with indicators like RSI or Bollinger Bands. Example: A sudden BTC pump at UTC 03:00 often signals Asian whales accumulating. Jump in early with a limit order on Binance’s spot market. ### 2. European Session (UTC 08:00–16:00) 🏰 [...] ### 2. European Session (UTC 08:00–16:00) 🏰 Who’s Trading? Europe’s retail and institutional players, including UK, Germany, and France. Binance’s EUR pairs shine here. What’s Hot?BTC/EUR, ETH/EUR, and stablecoin pairs like USDT/EUR get love. DeFi tokens often trend as Europe wakes up. Why It Matters: Liquidity ramps up, smoothing out spreads. EU regulatory news (e.g., MiCA updates) can move markets. Pro Tip: Use Binance’s Margin Trading f...
